heading: President’s Council on Innovation and Competitiveness
label: 3718 President’s Council on Innovation and Competitiveness
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the America Creating Opportunities to Meaningfully Promote Excellence in Technology, Education, and Science Act, also known as the America COMPETES Act, and not as part of the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 which comprises this chapter.

Designation of the Committee on Technology of the National Science and Technology Council To Carry Out Certain Requirements of the America COMPETES ActMemorandum of the President of the United States, , 73 F.R. 20523, provided:Apr. 10, 2008Memorandum for the Director of the Office of Science and Technology PolicyBy the authority vested in me as President of the United States by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including section 1006(e) of the America COMPETES Act () (the “Act”), I hereby designate the Committee on Technology of the National Science and Technology Council to carry out the responsibilities assigned to the Council on Innovation and Competitiveness in section 1006 of the Act.Public Law 110–69The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.George W. Bush.
amendments: Designation of the Committee on Technology of the National Science and Technology Council To Carry Out Certain Requirements of the America COMPETES ActMemorandum of the President of the United States, , 73 F.R. 20523, provided:Apr. 10, 2008Memorandum for the Director of the Office of Science and Technology PolicyBy the authority vested in me as President of the United States by the Constitution and the laws of the United States of America, including section 1006(e) of the America COMPETES Act () (the “Act”), I hereby designate the Committee on Technology of the National Science and Technology Council to carry out the responsibilities assigned to the Council on Innovation and Competitiveness in section 1006 of the Act.Public Law 110–69The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy is authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.George W. Bush.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The President shall establish a President’s Council on Innovation and Competitiveness.

monitoring implementation of public laws and initiatives for promoting innovation, including policies related to research funding, taxation, immigration, trade, and education that are proposed in this Act or in any other Act;

providing advice to the President with respect to global trends in competitiveness and innovation and allocation of Federal resources in education, job training, and technology research and development considering such global trends in competitiveness and innovation;

in consultation with the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, developing a process for using metrics to assess the impact of existing and proposed policies and rules that affect innovation capabilities in the United States;
identifying opportunities and making recommendations for the heads of executive agencies to improve innovation, monitoring, and reporting on the implementation of such recommendations;

developing metrics for measuring the progress of the Federal Government with respect to improving conditions for innovation, including through talent development, investment, and infrastructure improvements; and
submitting to the President and Congress an annual report on such progress.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 2 Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce shall serve as Chairperson of the Council.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 3 Coordination
children.text: The Chairperson of the Council shall ensure appropriate coordination between the Council and the National Economic Council, the National Security Council, and the National Science and Technology Council.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 4 Meetings
children.text: The Council shall meet on a semi-annual basis at the call of the Chairperson and the initial meeting of the Council shall occur not later than 6 months after .August 9, 2007

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Council shall develop a comprehensive agenda for strengthening the innovation and competitiveness capabilities of the Federal Government, State governments, academia, and the private sector in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: An assessment of current strengths and weaknesses of the United States investment in research and development.

children.label: B
children.text: Recommendations for addressing weaknesses and maintaining the United States as a world leader in research and development and technological innovation, including strategies for increasing the participation of individuals identified in section 1885a or 1885b of title 42 in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics fields.

children.label: C
children.text: Recommendations for strengthening the innovation and competitiveness capabilities of the Federal Government, State governments, academia, and the private sector in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: The private sector of the economy.

children.label: ii
children.text: Labor.

children.label: iii
children.text: Various fields including information technology, energy, engineering, high-technology manufacturing, health care, and education.

children.label: iv
children.text: Scientific organizations.

children.label: v
children.text: Academic organizations and other nongovernmental organizations working in the area of science or technology.

children.label: vi
children.text: Nongovernmental organizations, such as professional organizations, that represent individuals identified in section 1885a or 1885b of title 42 in the areas of science, engineering, technology, and mathematics.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: B Designation
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date that the National Academy of Sciences submits the list of recommended individuals to serve as advisors, the President shall designate 50 individuals to serve as advisors to the Council.

children.heading: Requirement to consult
children.label: C Requirement to consult
children.text: The Council shall develop the comprehensive agenda required by paragraph (1) in consultation with the advisors.

children.heading: Initial submission
children.label: A Initial submission
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Council shall submit to Congress and the President the comprehensive agenda required by paragraph (1).August 9, 2007

children.heading: Updates
children.label: B Updates
children.text: At least once every 2 years, the Council shall update the comprehensive agenda required by paragraph (1) and submit each such update to Congress and the President.

children.heading: Optional assignment
children.label: e Optional assignment
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (a) and paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (c), the President may designate an existing council to carry out the requirements of this section.
